.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:591:> **Search architecture:** The search pipeline uses a 4-stage architecture (candidate generation → fusion → reranking → filtering). Current retrieval uses five channels, normalizes fallback thresholds correctly, keeps disabled channels disabled through fallback, defers irreversible confidence truncation until after reranking, and enforces token budgets using actual post-truncation counts. Adaptive fusion includes an internal continuity profile (`semantic 0.52`, `keyword 0.18`, `recency 0.07`, `graph 0.23`), Stage 3 uses a minimum rerank gate of 4 candidates, the retained `applyLengthPenalty` input is a compatibility no-op, and `getRerankerStatus()` exposes reranker cache hits, misses, stale hits, and evictions. See [search/README.md](./mcp_server/lib/search/README.md) for pipeline details, scoring algorithms, and graph signal features.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:647:- **Memory causal trust display badges (012/005)** - `memory_search` results now carry an additive `trustBadges` payload per `MemoryResultEnvelope`: `confidence` from edge `strength`, `extractionAge`, `lastAccessAge`, `orphan` (no incoming edges), `weightHistoryChanged` (any connected edge has a `weight_history` row). Display-only — derived at response time from existing causal-edge columns; no schema change, no new relation types, no new storage of code/process/tool facts. Response profiles (`quick`, `research`, `resume`) preserve the badge payload on `results[]` and `topResult` (ADR-012-005)

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:751:**Claude lifecycle flow:** `UserPromptSubmit` covers prompt-time advisor delivery. `PreCompact -> SessionStart(compact)` handles cached context reinjection. On startup, the shared snapshot covers memory continuity, code-graph state, CocoIndex availability, and an explicit note that later structural reads may differ if the repo state changed. On resume, the runtime loads prior session state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:775:**Architecture:** CocoIndex (semantic, external MCP) finds code by concept. Code Graph (structural, this system) maps imports, calls, hierarchy. Memory (session, existing MCP) preserves decisions. The compact-merger combines all three under a 4000-token budget for compaction injection.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:789:**Context metadata:** `code_graph_context` success payloads carry structured `data.metadata.partialOutput` (`isPartial`, `reasons`, `omittedSections`, `omittedAnchors`, `truncatedText`) plus an explicit `deadlineMs` field so callers can distinguish a complete answer from one trimmed by deadline or budget pressure. `code_graph_status` exposes `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment confidence). CocoIndex seed fidelity preserves `score`, `snippet`, and range metadata through context resolution.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:797:**Query routing:** Structural queries (callers, imports, deps) -> `code_graph_query`. Semantic/concept queries -> CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`). Session/memory queries -> `memory_context`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:799:**CocoIndex seed bridge:** CocoIndex search results (file + line range) are accepted as seeds by `code_graph_context`, which resolves them to graph nodes and expands structurally. This combines "what resembles what" (CocoIndex) with "what connects to what" (Code Graph).

README.md:336:The Memory Engine is a local-first cognitive memory system built as an MCP server. `generate-context.js` updates canonical packet continuity and may emit supporting generated context artifacts inside the spec folder. Canonical continuity lives in the spec packet itself: use `/spec_kit:resume` as the recovery surface, then rebuild context in this order: `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> canonical spec docs. The MCP server indexes those packet-local sources with vector embeddings, BM25 and FTS5 full-text search, and `memory_match_triggers()` can still surface relevant prior context automatically when deeper retrieval is needed.
README.md:357:| **L7** | Maintenance | 10 | 1,000 | Index scans, async ingest, learning history, and graph/CocoIndex maintenance |
README.md:365:Every search checks five core channels at once, with CocoIndex available as a semantic code search bridge:
README.md:368:- **FTS5** - Full-text search on exact words and phrases.
README.md:369:- **BM25** - Keyword relevance scoring.
README.md:373:**Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)** combines results across channels so memories scoring well in multiple channels rise to the top. **Graph-first routing** dispatches structural queries to the Code Graph first, then CocoIndex for semantic code discovery, then the memory pipeline. A **3-tier FTS fallback** activates when graph and semantic channels miss: FTS5 full-text, BM25 keyword scoring, then Grep/Glob filesystem search. The system truncates weak results and ensures every active channel is represented.
README.md:376:#### Search Pipeline
README.md:381:- **Fusion** - RRF-based scoring with post-fusion signals such as co-activation, FSRS decay, interference control, intent weights, and graph/session boosts when enabled.
README.md:382:- **Rerank** - Cross-encoder reranking with chunk reassembly, a minimum Stage 3 gate of 4 candidates, and compatibility-only length-penalty wiring that resolves to a neutral `1.0` multiplier. `getRerankerStatus()` exposes latency plus cache hits, misses, stale hits, and evictions; if the reranker is unavailable, Stage 2 order is preserved with degraded metadata.
README.md:383:- **Filtering** - State/quality filtering, confidence annotation, token-budget enforcement, and final response shaping without mutating post-rerank scores.
README.md:508:### 🔍 CocoIndex + Code Graph
README.md:510:The framework uses two different code-understanding systems on purpose. **CocoIndex** handles semantic discovery, so the assistant can answer "find code that does X" or "how is Y implemented?" without knowing exact symbols first. The **Code Graph** handles structural expansion, so the assistant can answer questions like "what calls this?", "what imports this?", or "what breaks if we change it?" using an indexed relationship graph.
README.md:512:The intended routing order is graph-first: the code graph resolves structural queries first, CocoIndex finds semantic candidates when structural resolution misses, and Memory supports session decisions and active-task context after the packet-local recovery sources have been checked. A 3-tier FTS fallback escalates automatically when results are weak.
README.md:566:| **CocoIndex** | Concept search, similar implementations, unfamiliar modules | `mcp__cocoindex_code__search` |
README.md:569:| **CCC utilities** | CocoIndex availability, reindexing, result feedback | `ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, `ccc_feedback` |
README.md:574:The default routing order is: **Code Graph** (structural) -> **CocoIndex** (semantic code) -> **Memory** (session/decision context). This graph-first approach tries structural resolution before semantic similarity, with a 3-tier FTS fallback when earlier stages miss.
README.md:577:- Use **CocoIndex** for semantic and intent-based questions: "find code that validates memory quality", "show similar routing patterns", "where is the logic for X?"
README.md:832:- Search order: `match_triggers` → `memory_context` → `memory_search` → grep/glob
README.md:834:- Uses both CocoIndex semantic search and the 5-channel memory system. Read-only.
README.md:1078:- Diagnoses all 4 MCP servers (Spec Kit Memory, CocoIndex Code, Code Mode, Sequential Thinking) with PASS/WARN/FAIL per check
README.md:1188:- **Search Pipeline** - 5-channel retrieval, fallback routing, reranking, graph-walk rollout, confidence and token-budget policies.
README.md:1199:The runtime centers on a SQLite `memory_index` table with 56 columns plus companion FTS5/vector, lineage, checkpoint, working-memory, and eval tables.
README.md:1202:- **Search companions** - FTS5 and vector tables support lexical and embedding retrieval alongside BM25 rebuild/index data.
README.md:1259:A: Packet continuity and any supporting generated context artifacts use structured frontmatter and anchored markdown so the memory engine can classify, index, and retrieve them reliably. For recovery, start with `/spec_kit:resume` and the packet-local continuity ladder `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> canonical spec docs. After that, `memory_match_triggers()` can do a fast trigger/cognitive pass, while `memory_context()` and `memory_search()` handle deeper retrieval with intent routing, reranking, and filtering.
README.md:1279:A: 60 total across 4 native MCP servers, sourced from the registered MCP-dispatched tools only (internal helper handlers and any deferred / not-yet-wired handlers are intentionally excluded). The canonical count for the `spec_kit_memory` server is `TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length` in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts` (currently 51). All server bindings are defined in `opencode.json`.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Orchestrator
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// 4-stage pipeline execution with per-stage error handling and timeouts.
     5	//
     6	// B1 FIX: Each stage is wrapped in try/catch with withTimeout().
     7	// Stage 1 is mandatory (throws on failure — no candidates = no results).
     8	// Stages 2-4 fall back to previous stage output with degraded metadata.
     9	// Timing is recorded for latency observability.
    10	//
    11	// I/O CONTRACT:
    12	// Input:  PipelineConfig (query, embedding, limits, flags, intent, session)
    13	// Output: PipelineResult { results: Stage4ReadonlyRow[], metadata, annotations, trace }
    14	// Key invariants:
    15	//     - results are scored by Stage 2, reranked by Stage 3, filtered by Stage 4
    16	//     - Score fields are frozen after Stage 3; Stage 4 output scores == Stage 3 output scores
    17	//     - Stage metadata for all four stages is included for observability
    18	//     - When a stage degrades, metadata.degraded = true and timing tracks each stage
    19	// Side effects:
    20	//     - Delegates to each stage; see individual stage modules for their side effects
    21	
    22	import type {
    23	  PipelineConfig,
    24	  PipelineResult,
    25	  Stage1Output,
    26	  Stage2Output,
    27	  Stage3Output,
    28	  Stage4Output,
    29	  Stage4ReadonlyRow,
    30	  SignalStatus,
    31	} from './types.js';
    32	
    33	import { executeStage1 } from './stage1-candidate-gen.js';
    34	import { executeStage2 } from './stage2-fusion.js';
    35	import { executeStage3 } from './stage3-rerank.js';
    36	import { executeStage4 } from './stage4-filter.js';
    37	import { MemoryError, withTimeout } from '../../errors/core.js';
    38	
    39	// Feature catalog: 4-stage pipeline architecture
    40	// Feature catalog: 4-stage pipeline refactor
    41	
    42	
    43	/** Per-stage timeout in milliseconds. */
    44	const STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000;
    45	
    46	/**
    47	 * Execute the 4-stage retrieval pipeline with per-stage error handling.
    48	 *
    49	 * Stage 1: Candidate Generation — MANDATORY (throws on failure)
    50	 * Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration — falls back to unsorted candidates
    51	 * Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate — falls back to unranked scored results
    52	 * Stage 4: Filter + Annotate — falls back to unfiltered results
    53	 *
    54	 * @param config Pipeline configuration derived from search args
    55	 * @returns Pipeline result with stage metadata and timing
    56	 */
    57	export async function executePipeline(config: PipelineConfig): Promise<PipelineResult> {
    58	  const timing: Record<string, number> = {};
    59	  const pipelineStart = Date.now();
    60	  let degraded = false;
    61	
    62	  // -- Stage 1: Candidate Generation (MANDATORY — no fallback possible) --
    63	  let stage1Result: Stage1Output;
    64	  try {
    65	    const t0 = Date.now();
    66	    stage1Result = await withTimeout(
    67	      executeStage1({ config }),
    68	      STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    69	      'Stage 1: Candidate Generation',
    70	    );
    71	    timing.stage1 = Date.now() - t0;
    72	  } catch (err) {
    73	    throw new MemoryError(
    74	      'PIPELINE_STAGE1_FAILED',
    75	      `Candidate generation failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`,
    76	      { cause: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) },
    77	    );
    78	  }
    79	
    80	  // -- Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration (falls back to unsorted candidates) --
    81	  let stage2Result: Stage2Output;
    82	  try {
    83	    const t0 = Date.now();
    84	    stage2Result = await withTimeout(
    85	      executeStage2({
    86	        candidates: stage1Result.candidates,
    87	        config,
    88	        stage1Metadata: stage1Result.metadata,
    89	      }),
    90	      STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    91	      'Stage 2: Fusion',
    92	    );
    93	    timing.stage2 = Date.now() - t0;
    94	  } catch (err) {
    95	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
    96	    console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 2 failed, returning unscored candidates: ${msg}`);
    97	    degraded = true;
    98	    stage2Result = {
    99	      scored: stage1Result.candidates,
   100	      metadata: {
   101	        sessionBoostApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
   102	        causalBoostApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
   103	        intentWeightsApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
   104	        artifactRoutingApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
   105	        feedbackSignalsApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
   106	        qualityFiltered: 0,
   107	        durationMs: 0,
   108	      },
   109	    };
   110	    timing.stage2 = 0;
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  // -- Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate (falls back to unranked scored results) --
   114	  let stage3Result: Stage3Output;
   115	  try {
   116	    const t0 = Date.now();
   117	    stage3Result = await withTimeout(
   118	      executeStage3({
   119	        scored: stage2Result.scored,
   120	        config,
   121	      }),
   122	      STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
   123	      'Stage 3: Rerank',
   124	    );
   125	    timing.stage3 = Date.now() - t0;
   126	  } catch (err) {
   127	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   128	    console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 3 failed, returning unranked results: ${msg}`);
   129	    degraded = true;
   130	    stage3Result = {
   131	      reranked: stage2Result.scored,
   132	      metadata: {
   133	        rerankApplied: false,
   134	        chunkReassemblyStats: {
   135	          collapsedChunkHits: 0,
   136	          chunkParents: 0,
   137	          reassembled: 0,
   138	          fallback: 0,
   139	        },
   140	        durationMs: 0,
   141	      },
   142	    };
   143	    timing.stage3 = 0;
   144	  }
   145	
   146	  // -- Stage 4: Filter + Annotate (falls back to unfiltered results) --
   147	  let stage4Result: Stage4Output;
   148	  try {
   149	    const t0 = Date.now();
   150	    stage4Result = await withTimeout(
   151	      executeStage4({
   152	        results: stage3Result.reranked as Stage4ReadonlyRow[],
   153	        config,
   154	        stage1Metadata: { constitutionalInjected: stage1Result.metadata.constitutionalInjected },
   155	      }),
   156	      STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
   157	      'Stage 4: Filter',
   158	    );
   159	    timing.stage4 = Date.now() - t0;
   160	  } catch (err) {
   161	    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   162	    console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 4 failed, returning unfiltered results: ${msg}`);
   163	    degraded = true;
   164	    stage4Result = {
   165	      final: stage3Result.reranked as Stage4ReadonlyRow[],
   166	      metadata: {
   167	        stateFiltered: 0,
   168	        constitutionalInjected: stage1Result.metadata.constitutionalInjected,
   169	        evidenceGapDetected: false,
   170	        durationMs: 0,
   171	      },
   172	      annotations: {
   173	        stateStats: {},
   174	        featureFlags: {},
   175	      },
   176	    };
   177	    timing.stage4 = 0;
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  timing.total = Date.now() - pipelineStart;
   181	
   182	  return {
   183	    results: stage4Result.final,
   184	    metadata: {
   185	      stage1: stage1Result.metadata,
   186	      stage2: stage2Result.metadata,
   187	      stage3: stage3Result.metadata,
   188	      stage4: stage4Result.metadata,
   189	      timing,
   190	      degraded: degraded || undefined,
   191	    },
   192	    annotations: stage4Result.annotations,
   193	    trace: config.trace,
   194	  };
   195	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "Search Subsystem"
     3	description: "5-channel hybrid search architecture combining vector, lexical (BM25/FTS5), graph-based and structure-aware graph retrieval with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and Adaptive Fusion."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "search subsystem"
     6	  - "hybrid search"
     7	  - "vector search"
     8	---
     9	
    10	# Search Subsystem
    11	
    12	> 5-channel hybrid search architecture combining vector, lexical (BM25/FTS5), graph-based and structure-aware graph retrieval, fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and Adaptive Fusion.
    13	
    14	---
    15	
    16	## TABLE OF CONTENTS
    17	<!-- ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    18	
    19	- [1. OVERVIEW](#1-overview)
    20	  - [4-STAGE PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE](#4-stage-pipeline-architecture)
    21	- [2. KEY CONCEPTS](#2-key-concepts)
    22	- [3. MODULE STRUCTURE](#3-module-structure)
    23	- [4. FEATURES](#4-features)
    24	  - [GRAPH SIGNAL FEATURES](#graph-signal-features)
    25	  - [SAVE-TIME PROCESSING PIPELINE](#save-time-processing-pipeline)
    26	  - [SCORING ENHANCEMENTS](#scoring-enhancements)
    27	- [FTS CAPABILITY CASCADE FLOOR](#fts-capability-cascade-floor)
    28	- [5. USAGE EXAMPLES](#5-usage-examples)
    29	- [6. RECENT CHANGES (SPRINT 8)](#6-recent-changes-sprint-8)
    30	- [7. RELATED RESOURCES](#7-related-resources)
    31	
    32	<!-- /ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	## 1. OVERVIEW
    37	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    38	
    39	The search subsystem provides production-grade hybrid search capabilities with multiple retrieval methods fused via RRF scoring. It handles query expansion, intent classification, typo tolerance and optional cross-encoder reranking.
    40	
    41	Gate E alignment: retrieval is subordinate to the canonical continuity chain. Resume and continuity work should anchor on `/spec_kit:resume`, then rebuild context from `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`. Search helps rank supporting evidence after those source-of-truth surfaces are known.
    42	
    43	**Core Capabilities:**
    44	- **5-Channel Hybrid Search**: Vector (semantic) + BM25/FTS5 (lexical) + Graph (relationship-based) + Graph Structure (structural)
    45	- **RRF Score Fusion**: Industry-standard k=40 with convergence bonuses
    46	- **Intent Classification**: 7 intent types route to task-specific retrieval weights
    47	- **Query Enhancement**: Fuzzy matching (Levenshtein) + acronym expansions (via hybrid-search.ts inline logic)
    48	- **Reranking Pipeline**: Optional cross-encoder with length penalties
    49	- **MMR Diversity Reranking**: Maximal Marginal Relevance to reduce redundancy in result sets
    50	- **Evidence Gap Detection**: Identifies missing context and suggests follow-up queries
    51	- **Schema Management**: sqlite-vec schema v15 (current) with document-type fields, event-based decay and phase-aware columns
    52	
    53	**Architecture Pattern:**
    54	```text
    55	Query Input
    56	    |
    57	Intent Classifier -> Task-specific weights
    58	    |
    59	Parallel Search (5 channels)
    60	|---> Vector (sqlite-vec)       -> Semantic matches
    61	|---> BM25 (Pure JS)            -> Keyword matches
    62	|---> Graph (Co-activation)     -> Relationship matches
    63	|---> Graph Structure           -> Structural matches
    64	    |
    65	RRF Fusion (k=40) + Adaptive Fusion -> Unified scores
    66	    |
    67	MMR Diversity Reranking -> Redundancy reduction
    68	    |
    69	Cross-Encoder Rerank (optional) -> Relevance refinement
    70	    |
    71	Recency Boost + Co-activation -> Final adjustments
    72	    |
    73	Final Results
    74	```
    75	
    76	**Architecture Note:**
    77	`vector-index.ts` is the primary typed export surface for the vector index and re-exports the split schema, query, mutation, store and alias modules. `vector-index-impl.ts` is now a 14-line backward-compatibility shim that simply re-exports `vector-index.ts` for older import paths.
    78	
    79	<a id="4-stage-pipeline-architecture"></a>
    80	### 4-Stage Pipeline Architecture
    81	
    82	The search pipeline (R6) decomposes retrieval into four bounded stages with strict responsibilities. Each stage has clear input/output contracts defined in `pipeline/types.ts`.
    83	
    84	```text
    85	Stage 1                Stage 2                 Stage 3              Stage 4
    86	CANDIDATE GEN    -->   FUSION + SIGNALS   -->  RERANK + AGGREGATE  -->  FILTER + ANNOTATE
    87	(no score changes)     (single scoring point)  (score changes: YES)    (score changes: NO)
    88	```
    89	
    90	**Stage 1 — Candidate Generation** (`stage1-candidate-gen.ts`):
    91	Executes 5 search channels in parallel and collects raw candidates with no scoring modifications.
    92	
    93	| Channel | Source | Description |
    94	|---------|--------|-------------|
    95	| Vector | `vector-index.ts` | Semantic similarity via sqlite-vec through the split vector-index modules |
    96	| BM25 | `bm25-index.ts` | Pure TypeScript keyword matching |
    97	| FTS5 | `sqlite-fts.ts` | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring |
    98	| Graph | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Causal edge traversal + typed-weighted degree (R4) |
    99	| Degree | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Structural graph discovery via typed-weighted degree |
   100	
   101	Post-channel: constitutional memory injection, quality score filtering, tier/contextType filtering.
   102	
   103	**Stage 2 — Fusion + Signal Integration** (`stage2-fusion.ts`):
   104	Single authoritative point for ALL scoring signals. Signal application order is fixed:
   105	
   106	1. Session boost — working-memory attention amplification
   107	2. Causal boost — graph-traversal neighbor amplification
   108	3. Community co-retrieval (N2c) — inject community co-members
   109	4. Graph signals (N2a + N2b) — momentum + causal depth
   110	5. Testing effect — FSRS strengthening write-back (opt-in via `trackAccess`)
   111	6. Intent weights — non-hybrid only (G2 double-weighting prevention: `isHybrid` boolean guard)
   112	7. Artifact routing — class-based weight boosts
   113	8. Feedback signals — learned trigger boosts (0.7x weight) + negative demotions
   114	9. Anchor metadata — extract named ANCHOR sections (annotation only)
   115	10. Validation signals — quality scoring multiplier (clamped 0.8-1.2)
   116	
   117	**Stage 3 — Rerank + Aggregate** (`stage3-rerank.ts`):
   118	Cross-encoder reranking (optional, min 2 results) followed by MPAB chunk-to-memory aggregation. Aggregation formula: `parentScore = sMax + 0.3 * Sum(rest) / sqrt(N)` where `sMax` is the best chunk score and N is the remaining chunk count. Chunk ordering preserves `chunk_index` document order (B2 guarantee). `contentSource` metadata marks provenance (`reassembled_chunks` or `file_read_fallback`).
   119	
   120	**Stage 4 — Filter + Annotate** (`stage4-filter.ts`):
   121	**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering, per-tier limits, evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation. Lowest-priority retained-history rows remain fallback evidence only when filters allow them.
   122	
   123	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
   124	
   125	---
   126	
   127	## 2. KEY CONCEPTS
   128	<!-- ANCHOR:key-concepts -->
   129	
   130	### Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
   131	
   132	**Formula**: `score = Sum 1/(k + rank_i)` where k=40 (tuned for ~1000-memory corpus)
   133	
   134	**Why RRF?**
   135	- Parameter-free fusion (no weight tuning required)
   136	- Resilient to retrieval method failures (graceful degradation)
   137	- Citation: Cormack et al. "RRF outperforms Condorcet" (SIGIR 2009)
   138	
   139	**Enhancements (REQ-011):**
   140	- **10% Convergence Bonus**: Results in multiple sources get +10% score boost
   141	- **1.5x Graph Weight**: Graph-exclusive discoveries weighted higher for novelty
   142	- **Adaptive Fusion**: Intent-aware weighted RRF with opt-in evaluation mode (feature flag `SPECKIT_ADAPTIVE_FUSION`)
   143	- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results
   144	- **Recency Boost**: Time-aware score adjustment favoring recently updated memories
   145	- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal
   146	
   147	**Example:**
   148	```javascript
   149	// Vector rank: 2, BM25 rank: 5, Graph rank: 1
   150	// RRF score = 1/(40+2) + 1/(40+5) + 1.5/(40+1)
   151	//           = 0.0238 + 0.0222 + 0.0366 = 0.0826
   152	// Convergence bonus: 0.0826 * 1.10 = 0.0909 (final)
   153	```
   154	
   155	### BM25 (Best Matching 25)
   156	
   157	**Formula**:
   158	```text
   159	score(D, Q) = Sum IDF(qi) * (tf(qi,D) * (k1+1)) / (tf(qi,D) + k1 * (1-b + b*|D|/avgdl))
   160	```
   161	
   162	**Parameters:**
   163	- `k1 = 1.2`: Term frequency saturation (higher = less saturation)
   164	- `b = 0.75`: Length normalization (0=ignore length, 1=full penalty)
   165	- `tf(qi,D)`: Term frequency of query term qi in document D
   166	- `|D|`: Document length, `avgdl`: Average document length
   167	- `IDF(qi)`: Inverse document frequency: `log((N - n(qi) + 0.5) / (n(qi) + 0.5) + 1)`
   168	
   169	**Why BM25?**
   170	- Handles term frequency saturation (repeated words don't dominate)
   171	- Length normalization (short docs not penalized unfairly)
   172	- Pure JavaScript implementation (REQ-028, no Python dependency)
   173	
   174	<a id="fts-capability-cascade-floor"></a>
   175	### FTS Capability Cascade Floor
   176	
   177	Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:
   178	
   179	- `lexicalPath`: the lane that actually ran for lexical retrieval. Current packet-owned values are `fts5` and `unavailable`. The wider response schema also allows `like`, but packet `010` does not claim that lane for this runtime seam.
   180	- `fallbackState`: the truthful FTS capability outcome for the request.
   181	
   182	The forced-degrade matrix is:
   183	
   184	| `fallbackState` | Meaning | `lexicalPath` |
   185	|-----------------|---------|---------------|
   186	| `ok` | FTS5 compile probe passed, `memory_fts` exists, and BM25 ranking executed normally | `fts5` |
   187	| `compile_probe_miss` | `PRAGMA compile_options` does not report FTS5 support, so lexical work cannot run for this request | `unavailable` |
   188	| `missing_table` | FTS5 support is present, but `memory_fts` is missing at runtime | `unavailable` |
   189	| `no_such_module_fts5` | The SQLite engine rejects FTS5 usage with `no such module: fts5` | `unavailable` |
   190	| `bm25_runtime_failure` | The FTS5 table exists, but the `bm25(...)` ranking call fails at runtime | `unavailable` |
   191	
   192	The contract is intentionally narrow: preserve result-shape semantics, surface truthful lane metadata, and let later packets build on that truth instead of inferring capability from empty results or warning logs.
   193	
   194	### Intent-Aware Retrieval
   195	
   196	**7 Intent Types** (REQ-012, T036-T039):
   197	
   198	| Intent           | Description                  | Prioritizes                         |
   199	| ---------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
   200	| `add_feature`    | Building new functionality   | Patterns, examples, architecture    |
   201	| `fix_bug`        | Debugging issues             | Error history, root cause, patches  |
   202	| `refactor`       | Restructuring code           | Patterns, dependencies, design docs |
   203	| `security_audit` | Security review              | Vulnerabilities, audit logs         |
   204	| `understand`     | Learning/exploring (default) | Explanations, context, decisions    |
   205	| `find_spec`      | Spec document retrieval      | Boosts spec-doc source weights      |
   206	| `find_decision`  | Decision rationale lookup    | Boosts decision-record source weights |
   207	
   208	**Detection**: Keyword matching with primary (2x weight) and secondary (1x weight) terms.
   209	
   210	**Example**:
   211	```javascript
   212	// Query: "add user registration feature"
   213	// Intent: add_feature
   214	// Boosts: architecture memories, pattern docs, examples
   215	```
   216	
   217	### Cross-Encoder Reranking
   218	
   219	**Purpose**: Refine top results using query-document pair scoring.
   220

   193	function normalizeDocumentType(value: unknown): string | null {
   194	  if (typeof value !== 'string') {
   195	    return null;
   196	  }
   197	  const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
   198	  return normalized.length > 0 ? normalized : null;
   199	}
   200	
   201	function resolveAnchorId(row: Record<string, unknown>): string | null {
   202	  const value = row.anchor_id ?? row.anchorId;
   203	  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0
   204	    ? value.trim()
   205	    : null;
   206	}
   207	
   208	function resolveFilePath(row: Record<string, unknown>): string | null {
   209	  const value = row.file_path ?? row.filePath;
   210	  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0
   211	    ? value
   212	    : null;
   213	}
   214	
   215	function resolveCanonicalSourceKind(row: Record<string, unknown>): CanonicalSourceKind | null {
   216	  const documentType = normalizeDocumentType(row.document_type ?? row.documentType);
   217	  const importanceTier = normalizeDocumentType(row.importance_tier ?? row.importanceTier);
   218	  const anchorId = resolveAnchorId(row);
   219	
   220	  if (importanceTier === 'constitutional' || documentType === 'constitutional') {

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Stage1 Candidate Gen
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// 4-stage retrieval pipeline architecture
     5	//
     6	// Responsibility: Execute search channels and collect raw candidate results.
     7	// This stage avoids downstream fusion/reranking, but may apply temporal
     8	// Contiguity to raw vector-channel hits before later pipeline stages.
     9	// Results are collected from the appropriate search channel based on search type.
    10	//
    11	// Search channels handled:
    12	//   - multi-concept: Generate per-concept embeddings, run multiConceptSearch
    13	//   - hybrid (deep mode): Query expansion + multi-variant hybrid search + dedup
    14	//   - hybrid (R12):       Embedding-based query expansion (SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_EXPANSION)
    15	// Suppressed when R15 classifies query as "simple" (mutual exclusion)
    16	//   - hybrid: collectRawCandidates → falls back to vector on failure
    17	//   - vector: Direct vectorSearch
    18	//
    19	// Post-channel operations:
    20	//   - Constitutional memory injection (if not already present)
    21	//   - Quality score filtering
    22	//   - Tier and contextType filtering
    23	//
    24	// I/O CONTRACT:
    25	// Input:  Stage1Input { config: PipelineConfig }
    26	// Output: Stage1Output { candidates: PipelineRow[], metadata }
    27	// Key invariants:
    28	//     - candidates contains raw channel scores; vector hits may include an
    29	//       optional temporal-contiguity boost applied before downstream fusion
    30	//     - Constitutional rows are always present when includeConstitutional=true and no tier filter
    31	//     - All rows pass qualityThreshold (if set) and tier/contextType filters
    32	// Side effects:
    33	//     - Generates query embeddings via the embeddings provider (external call)
    34	//     - Reads from the vector index and FTS5 / BM25 index (DB reads only)
    35	//
    36	import type { Stage1Input, Stage1Output, PipelineRow } from './types.js';
    37	import { resolveEffectiveScore } from './types.js';
    38	import * as vectorIndex from '../vector-index.js';
    39	import * as embeddings from '../../providers/embeddings.js';
    40	import * as hybridSearch from '../hybrid-search.js';
    41	import { vectorSearchWithContiguity } from '../../cognitive/temporal-contiguity.js';
    42	import { isMultiQueryEnabled, isEmbeddingExpansionEnabled, isMemorySummariesEnabled, isQueryDecompositionEnabled, isGraphConceptRoutingEnabled, isLlmReformulationEnabled, isHyDEEnabled, isQuerySurrogatesEnabled, isTemporalContiguityEnabled, isQueryConceptExpansionEnabled } from '../search-flags.js';
    43	import { expandQuery } from '../query-expander.js';
    44	import { expandQueryWithEmbeddings, isExpansionActive } from '../embedding-expansion.js';
    45	import { querySummaryEmbeddings, checkScaleGate } from '../memory-summaries.js';

    89	// -- Helper Functions --
    90	
    91	/**
    92	 * Filter results by a minimum quality score threshold.
    93	 *
    94	 * - If no threshold is provided or it is not a finite number, all results pass.
    95	 * - Threshold is clamped to [0, 1].
    96	 * - Rows with a missing or non-finite `quality_score` are treated as 0.
    97	 *
    98	 * @param results - Candidate rows to filter.
    99	 * @param threshold - Minimum quality score in [0, 1] (inclusive).
   100	 * @returns Filtered array; original array returned unchanged when no threshold applies.
   101	 */
   102	function filterByMinQualityScore(
   103	  results: PipelineRow[],
   104	  threshold?: number
   105	): PipelineRow[] {
   106	  if (typeof threshold !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(threshold)) {
   107	    return results;
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const clampedThreshold = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, threshold));
   111	
   112	  return results.filter((row) => {
   113	    const rawScore = row.quality_score as number | undefined;
   114	    const score =
   115	      typeof rawScore === 'number' && Number.isFinite(rawScore) ? rawScore : 0;
   116	    return score >= clampedThreshold;
   117	  });
   118	}
   119	
   120	function backfillMissingQualityScores(results: PipelineRow[]): PipelineRow[] {
   121	  return results.map((row) => {
   122	    if (row.quality_score !== 0 && row.quality_score != null) {
   123	      return row;
   124	    }
   125	
   126	    return {
   127	      ...row,
   128	      quality_score: computeBackfillQualityScore(row),
   129	    };
   130	  });
   131	}
   132	
   133	function applyArchiveFilter(
   134	  results: PipelineRow[],
   135	  _includeArchived: boolean
   136	): PipelineRow[] {
   137	  return results;
   138	}
   139	
   140	function applyFolderFilter(
   141	  results: PipelineRow[],
   142	  specFolder?: string
   143	): PipelineRow[] {
   144	  if (!specFolder) return results;
   145	  return results.filter((row) => {
   146	    const rowSpecFolder = row.spec_folder ?? row.specFolder;
   147	    return rowSpecFolder === specFolder;
   148	  });
   149	}
   150	
   151	function applyTierFilter(
   152	  results: PipelineRow[],
   153	  tier?: string
   154	): PipelineRow[] {
   155	  if (!tier) return results;
   156	  return results.filter((row) => row.importance_tier === tier);
   157	}
   158	
   159	/**
   160	 * Resolve the effective context type from a pipeline row.
   161	 *
   162	 * Rows may carry context type under either `contextType` (camelCase) or
   163	 * `context_type` (snake_case). This function normalises the lookup.
   164	 *
   165	 * @param row - The pipeline row to inspect.
   166	 * @returns The context type string, or `undefined` if absent.
   167	 */
   168	function resolveRowContextType(row: PipelineRow): string | undefined {
   169	  if (typeof row.contextType === 'string' && row.contextType.length > 0) {
   170	    return row.contextType;
   171	  }
   172	  if (typeof row.context_type === 'string' && row.context_type.length > 0) {
   173	    return row.context_type;
   174	  }
   175	  return undefined;
   176	}
   177	
   178	function mergeStringLists(...values: unknown[]): string[] {
   179	  const merged = new Set<string>();
   180	  for (const value of values) {
   181	    if (!Array.isArray(value)) {
   182	      continue;
   183	    }
   184	    for (const entry of value) {
   185	      if (typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0) {
   186	        merged.add(entry);
   187	      }
   188	    }
   189	  }
   190	  return Array.from(merged);
   191	}
   192	
   193	function readFiniteScoreMap(value: unknown): Record<string, number> {
   194	  if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) {
   195	    return {};
   196	  }
   197	
   198	  const normalized: Record<string, number> = {};
   199	  for (const [key, score] of Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)) {
   200	    if (typeof score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(score)) {
   201	      normalized[key] = score;
   202	    }
   203	  }
   204	  return normalized;
   205	}
   206	
   207	function readQualityFlags(value: unknown): string[] {
   208	  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
   209	    return value.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0);
   210	  }
   211	  if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
   212	    return [];
   213	  }
   214	  try {
   215	    const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
   216	    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
   217	      return parsed.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0);
   218	    }
   219	  } catch {
   220	    // Fall back to comma-delimited parsing for legacy callers/tests.
   221	  }
   222	  return value
   223	    .split(',')
   224	    .map((entry) => entry.trim())
   225	    .filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
   226	}
   227	
   228	function isMigratedGraphMetadataCandidate(row: PipelineRow): boolean {
   229	  const migratedValue = row.migrated ?? row.is_migrated ?? row.metadata_migrated;
   230	  if (migratedValue === true || migratedValue === 1 || migratedValue === '1' || migratedValue === 'true') {
   231	    return true;
   232	  }
   233	  const qualityFlags = readQualityFlags(row.quality_flags ?? row.qualityFlags);
   234	  return qualityFlags.includes(GRAPH_METADATA_MIGRATED_QUALITY_FLAG);
   235	}
   236	
   237	function mergeScoreMaps(...maps: Array<Record<string, number>>): Record<string, number> {
   238	  const merged: Record<string, number> = {};
   239	  for (const map of maps) {
   240	    for (const [key, score] of Object.entries(map)) {
   241	      if (!(key in merged) || score > merged[key]!) {
   242	        merged[key] = score;
   243	      }
   244	    }
   245	  }
   246	  return merged;
   247	}
   248	
   249	function getCandidateSources(row: PipelineRow): string[] {
   250	  const explicitSources = mergeStringLists(row.sources);
   251	  if (explicitSources.length > 0) {
   252	    return explicitSources;
   253	  }
   254	  return typeof row.source === 'string' && row.source.length > 0 ? [row.source] : [];
   255	}
   256	
   257	function getCandidateSourceScores(row: PipelineRow): Record<string, number> {
   258	  const sourceScores = readFiniteScoreMap(row.sourceScores);
   259	  if (Object.keys(sourceScores).length > 0) {
   260	    return sourceScores;

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Stage2 Fusion
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// the rollout (R6): 4-Stage Retrieval Pipeline
     5	//
     6	// I/O CONTRACT:
     7	// Input:  Stage2Input { candidates: PipelineRow[], config, stage1Metadata }
     8	// Output: Stage2Output { scored: PipelineRow[], metadata }
     9	// Key invariants:
    10	//     - Every score modification in the pipeline happens exactly once here
    11	//     - Intent weights are NEVER applied to hybrid results (G2 double-weighting guard)
    12	//     - scored is sorted descending by effective composite score on exit
    13	// Side effects:
    14	//     - FSRS write-back to memory_index (when trackAccess=true) — DB write
    15	//     - Learned trigger and negative-feedback reads from DB
    16	//
    17	// PURPOSE: Single point for ALL scoring signals. Intent weights are
    18	// Applied ONCE here only — this is the architectural guard against
    19	// The G2 double-weighting recurrence bug.
    20	//
    21	// SIGNAL APPLICATION ORDER (must not be reordered — 13 steps):
    22	// 1.  Session boost           — working-memory attention amplification
    23	// 1a. Recency fusion          — time-decay bonus for recent memories
    24	// 2.  Causal boost            — graph-traversal neighbor amplification
    25	// 2a. Co-activation spreading — spreading activation from top-N seeds
    26	// 2b. Community co-retrieval  — N2c inject community co-members
    27	// 2c. Graph signals           — N2a momentum + N2b causal depth
    28	// 3.  Testing effect          — FSRS strengthening write-back (trackAccess)
    29	// 4.  Intent weights          — non-hybrid search post-scoring adjustment
    30	// 5.  Artifact routing        — class-based weight boosts
    31	// 6.  Feedback signals        — learned trigger boosts + negative demotions
    32	// 7.  Artifact limiting       — result count cap from routing strategy
    33	// 8.  Anchor metadata         — extract named ANCHOR sections (annotation)
    34	// 9.  Validation metadata     — spec quality signals enrichment + quality scoring
    35	//
    36	// Hybrid search already applies intent-aware scoring
    37	// Internally (RRF / RSF fusion). Post-search intent weighting is
    38	// Therefore ONLY applied for non-hybrid search types (vector,
    39	// Multi-concept). Applying it to hybrid results would double-count.
    40	//
    41	// SCORE AUDIT CONTRACT: Stage 2 writes the fused `score` field (steps 1-7).
    42	// Stage 3 (rerank) MAY overwrite `score` with the reranked value and MUST
    43	// preserve the original in `stage2Score` for auditability (see F2.02 fix).
    44	// Stage 4 (filter) MUST NOT mutate any score fields — it is read-only.
    45	// The canonical reranker output is `rerankerScore`; `score` is synced to it
    46	// for downstream consumer compatibility.
    47

   124	const SPREAD_ACTIVATION_TOP_N = 5;
   125	const DEFAULT_LEARNED_STAGE2_MODEL_RELATIVE_PATH = path.join('models', 'learned-stage2-combiner.json');
   126	
   127	/** Recency fusion weight — controls how much recency score contributes to the fused score.
   128	 *  Env-tunable via SPECKIT_RECENCY_FUSION_WEIGHT (default 0.07). */
   129	const RECENCY_FUSION_WEIGHT = parseFloat(process.env.SPECKIT_RECENCY_FUSION_WEIGHT || '') || 0.07;
   130	
   131	/** Recency fusion cap — maximum bonus a candidate can receive from recency fusion.
   132	 *  Env-tunable via SPECKIT_RECENCY_FUSION_CAP (default 0.10). */
   133	const RECENCY_FUSION_CAP = parseFloat(process.env.SPECKIT_RECENCY_FUSION_CAP || '') || 0.10;
   134	
   135	const MIN_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER = 0.8;
   136	const MAX_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER = 1.2;
   137	
   138	type LearnedStage2ModelCacheEntry = {
   139	  path: string;
   140	  model: LearnedModel | null;
   141	};
   142	
   143	let cachedLearnedStage2Model: LearnedStage2ModelCacheEntry | null = null;
   144	let learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise: Promise<LearnedStage2ModelCacheEntry> | null = null;
   145	let learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath: string | null = null;
   146	
   147	function clampMultiplier(value: number): number {
   148	  if (!Number.isFinite(value)) return 1;
   149	  if (value < MIN_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER) return MIN_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER;
   150	  if (value > MAX_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER) return MAX_VALIDATION_MULTIPLIER;
   151	  return value;
   152	}
   153	
   154	function isShadowLearningModelLoadEnabled(): boolean {
   155	  return process.env.SPECKIT_SHADOW_LEARNING?.toLowerCase().trim() === 'true';
   156	}
   157	
   158	function resolveLearnedStage2ModelPath(): string {
   159	  const configured = process.env.SPECKIT_LEARNED_STAGE2_MODEL?.trim();
   160	  if (!configured) {
   161	    return path.resolve(process.cwd(), DEFAULT_LEARNED_STAGE2_MODEL_RELATIVE_PATH);
   162	  }
   163	  if (path.isAbsolute(configured)) {
   164	    return configured;
   165	  }
   166	  return path.resolve(process.cwd(), configured);
   167	}
   168	
   169	async function loadPersistedLearnedStage2Model(): Promise<LearnedModel | null> {
   170	  if (!isShadowLearningModelLoadEnabled()) {
   171	    return null;
   172	  }
   173	
   174	  const modelPath = resolveLearnedStage2ModelPath();
   175	  if (cachedLearnedStage2Model?.path === modelPath) {
   176	    return cachedLearnedStage2Model.model;
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  if (learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise && learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath === modelPath) {
   180	    return (await learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise).model;
   181	  }
   182	
   183	  if (learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath !== modelPath) {
   184	    learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise = null;
   185	    learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath = null;
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath = modelPath;
   189	  learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise = (async (): Promise<LearnedStage2ModelCacheEntry> => {
   190	    try {
   191	      const json = await readFile(modelPath, 'utf8');
   192	      const model = loadModel(json);
   193	      if (!model) {
   194	        console.warn(`[stage2-fusion] learned stage2 model at ${modelPath} is invalid; shadow scoring will use manual-only fallback`);
   195	      }
   196	      const entry = { path: modelPath, model };
   197	      cachedLearnedStage2Model = entry;
   198	      return entry;
   199	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   200	      const entry = { path: modelPath, model: null };
   201	      cachedLearnedStage2Model = entry;
   202	
   203	      const code = typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'code' in err
   204	        ? String((err as { code?: unknown }).code)
   205	        : '';
   206	      if (code !== 'ENOENT') {
   207	        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   208	        console.warn(`[stage2-fusion] learned stage2 model load failed: ${message}`);
   209	      }
   210	      return entry;
   211	    } finally {
   212	      learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise = null;
   213	      learnedStage2ModelLoadPromisePath = null;
   214	    }
   215	  })();
   216	
   217	  return (await learnedStage2ModelLoadPromise).model;
   218	}

    39	<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->
    40	## What Was Built
    41	
    42	This phase implemented six retrieval-quality fixes across context routing, folder discovery behavior, token budget handling, and intent classification. The changes targeted false zero-result outcomes and low-quality intent auto-detection paths.
    43	
    44	### Retrieval Quality Remediation
    45	
    46	The implementation updated strategy intent propagation, added recovery for over-narrow folder scope, introduced adaptive truncation behavior, wired folder boost scoring metadata, added two-tier metadata/content response behavior, and added a confidence floor that defaults weak auto-detections to `understand`.
    47	
    48	### Files Changed
    49	
    50	| File | Action | Purpose |
    51	|------|--------|---------|
    52	| `mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts` | Modified | Intent propagation, folder recovery, adaptive truncation, two-tier behavior |
    53	| `mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` | Modified | Folder boost application + intent confidence floor |
    54	| `mcp_server/lib/search/folder-discovery.ts` | Modified | Folder discovery support for scoring signal integration |
    55	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->

    69

    71	|----------|-----|
    72	| Keep recovery logic for narrow folder scope while introducing folder boost signal | Preserves safe fallback behavior for known 0-result edge case |
    73	| Apply confidence floor only to auto-detected intents | Explicit caller intent should not be overridden |
    74	| Separate structural doc remediation from runtime verification claims | Prevents overstating confidence when cache-sensitive runtime checks are pending |

    23	# Implementation Summary: FTS5 Fix, Search Dashboard, and DB Path Drift Fix
    24	<!-- HVR_REFERENCE: .opencode/skill/sk-doc/references/hvr_rules.md -->
    25	
    26	---
    27	
    28	<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->
    29	## Metadata
    30	
    31	| Field | Value |
    32	|-------|-------|
    33	| **Spec Folder** | 013-fts5-fix-and-search-dashboard |
    34	| **Completed** | 2026-04-01 — All 4 items implemented and verified |
    35	| **Level** | 2 |
    36	<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->
    37	
    38	---
    39	
    40	<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->
    41	## What Was Built
    42	
    43	All 4 items implemented, compiled, and verified at runtime.
    44	
    45	### Item 1 — FTS5 Double-Quoting Fix
    46	
    47	Multi-word searches now return FTS5-backed results. A guard at `sqlite-fts.ts` line 58 prevents tokens already wrapped in double quotes from being re-quoted, eliminating the invalid `""phrase""` syntax that caused SQLite to silently drop all FTS5 results for multi-word queries.
    48	
    49	### Item 2 — Search Dashboard Redesign
    50	
    51	The `/memory:search` output groups results by spec folder using leaf folder names (Design 10: folder-as-tree-group). Paths are no longer rendered at full length, making results scannable. Applied to both `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` and `.agents/commands/memory/search.toml`.
    52	
    53	### Item 3 — DB Path Drift Fix (4-layer defense-in-depth)
    54	
    55	Root cause: `resolve_database_path()` in `vector-index-store.ts` drifted to empty provider-specific databases after Voyage-4 lazy initialization. Four independent fixes applied:
    56	
    57	1. **Path stabilization** (`vector-index-store.ts` lines 277-289): `resolve_database_path()` validates against drift — detects and logs when provider would derive a different path than the one already in use.
    58	2. **Empty DB rebind guard** (`db-state.ts` lines 294-310): `reinitializeDatabase()` refuses to rebind consumers to an empty database (0 memories). Override available via `SPECKIT_FORCE_REBIND=true`.
    59	3. **Startup health check** (`context-server.ts` lines 1368-1374): Database path logged on startup; initialization sequence confirms DB is populated before consumers activate.
    60	4. **Connection caching** (`vector-index-store.ts` lines 763-771): Connections cached post-validation only, preventing stale references to drifted paths.
    61	
    62	### Item 4 — Silent Failure Remediation
    63	
    64	31+ warning logs added across 5 search pipeline files. Every `return []` or `return null` on unexpected conditions now logs a `console.warn` with `[module-name]` prefix before returning. Affected files: `hybrid-search.ts` (8+), `stage1-candidate-gen.ts` (11+), `vector-index-queries.ts` (12+). FTS scope filter updated to match exact-or-descendant folders via `LIKE ? || "/%"` pattern at `sqlite-fts.ts` lines 63-65.
    65	
    66	### Files Changed
    67	
    68	| File | Action | Purpose |
    69	|------|--------|---------|
    70	| `mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts` | Modified | FTS5 double-quote guard + descendant scope filter |
    71	| `mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts` | Modified | Stable `resolve_database_path()`, connection caching |
    72	| `mcp_server/core/db-state.ts` | Modified | Empty DB rebind guard in `reinitializeDatabase()` |
    73	| `mcp_server/context-server.ts` | Modified | Startup health check + DB path logging |
    74	| `mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts` | Modified | 8+ warning logs on silent failure paths |

    76	| `mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts` | Modified | 12+ warning logs on failure paths |
    77	| `.opencode/command/memory/search.md` | Modified | Design 10 dashboard applied |

   129	<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->
   130	## Known Limitations
   131	
   132	1. **SPECKIT_FORCE_REBIND bypass**: The empty DB rebind guard can be overridden with `SPECKIT_FORCE_REBIND=true`. This is intentional for edge cases where provider-specific DBs are deliberately used, but could mask legitimate drift if set permanently.
   133	2. **Warning log volume**: 31+ new warning logs fire only on actual failure paths, not normal operation. If a deployment has persistent misconfiguration, logs may be noisy until the root cause is fixed.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba README.md | sed -n '336,386p'; nl -ba README.md | sed -n '508,586p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   336	The Memory Engine is a local-first cognitive memory system built as an MCP server. `generate-context.js` updates canonical packet continuity and may emit supporting generated context artifacts inside the spec folder. Canonical continuity lives in the spec packet itself: use `/spec_kit:resume` as the recovery surface, then rebuild context in this order: `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> canonical spec docs. The MCP server indexes those packet-local sources with vector embeddings, BM25 and FTS5 full-text search, and `memory_match_triggers()` can still surface relevant prior context automatically when deeper retrieval is needed.
   337	
   338	`/memory:save` refreshes packet metadata on every invocation, and `session_resume` binds `args.sessionId` to transport caller context by default; set `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` for rollout canaries. Copilot, Claude, and Gemini all share the same compact-cache provenance path.
   339	
   340	The memory engine includes a compact code graph and session lifecycle surfaces alongside hybrid retrieval.
   341	
   342	The full MCP API reference is in the [MCP Server README](.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md).
   343	
   344	&nbsp;
   345	#### Layered MCP Surface
   346	
   347	The MCP tools are organized into a layered architecture. Each layer has a token budget that controls how much context it consumes:
   348	
   349	| Layer | Name | Tools | Token Budget | Purpose |
   350	|-------|------|-------|-------------|---------|
   351	| **L1** | Orchestration | 3 | 2,000 | Unified context, resume, and bootstrap entry points |
   352	| **L2** | Core | 4 | 1,500 | Search, quick search, trigger matching, save |
   353	| **L3** | Discovery | 4 | 800 | List, stats, health checks, and session readiness |
   354	| **L4** | Mutation | 4 | 500 | Delete, update, validate, bulk cleanup |
   355	| **L5** | Lifecycle | 4 | 600 | Checkpoints and lifecycle state |
   356	| **L6** | Analysis | 10 | 1,200 | Causal graph, epistemic baselines, evaluations, and dashboards |
   357	| **L7** | Maintenance | 10 | 1,000 | Index scans, async ingest, learning history, and graph/CocoIndex maintenance |
   358	| | **Total** | **47** | **8,400** | |
   359	
   360	Lower layers load only when needed. L1 is always available. L2 loads for any search. L3-L7 load based on the specific command being used.
   361	
   362	&nbsp;
   363	#### Hybrid Search
   364	
   365	Every search checks five core channels at once, with CocoIndex available as a semantic code search bridge:
   366	
   367	- **Vector** - Semantic similarity via embeddings. Finds related content when words differ.
   368	- **FTS5** - Full-text search on exact words and phrases.
   369	- **BM25** - Keyword relevance scoring.
   370	- **Causal Graph** - Follows cause-and-effect links between memories.
   371	- **Degree** - Scores by graph connectivity, weighted by edge type.
   372	
   373	**Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)** combines results across channels so memories scoring well in multiple channels rise to the top. **Graph-first routing** dispatches structural queries to the Code Graph first, then CocoIndex for semantic code discovery, then the memory pipeline. A **3-tier FTS fallback** activates when graph and semantic channels miss: FTS5 full-text, BM25 keyword scoring, then Grep/Glob filesystem search. The system truncates weak results and ensures every active channel is represented.
   374	
   375	&nbsp;
   376	#### Search Pipeline
   377	
   378	Every search passes through 4 stages:
   379	
   380	- **Candidate generation** - Parallel retrieval from the active channels plus constitutional injection where applicable.
   381	- **Fusion** - RRF-based scoring with post-fusion signals such as co-activation, FSRS decay, interference control, intent weights, and graph/session boosts when enabled.
   382	- **Rerank** - Cross-encoder reranking with chunk reassembly, a minimum Stage 3 gate of 4 candidates, and compatibility-only length-penalty wiring that resolves to a neutral `1.0` multiplier. `getRerankerStatus()` exposes latency plus cache hits, misses, stale hits, and evictions; if the reranker is unavailable, Stage 2 order is preserved with degraded metadata.
   383	- **Filtering** - State/quality filtering, confidence annotation, token-budget enforcement, and final response shaping without mutating post-rerank scores.
   384	
   385	&nbsp;
   386	#### Query Intelligence
   508	### 🔍 CocoIndex + Code Graph
   509	
   510	The framework uses two different code-understanding systems on purpose. **CocoIndex** handles semantic discovery, so the assistant can answer "find code that does X" or "how is Y implemented?" without knowing exact symbols first. The **Code Graph** handles structural expansion, so the assistant can answer questions like "what calls this?", "what imports this?", or "what breaks if we change it?" using an indexed relationship graph.
   511	
   512	The intended routing order is graph-first: the code graph resolves structural queries first, CocoIndex finds semantic candidates when structural resolution misses, and Memory supports session decisions and active-task context after the packet-local recovery sources have been checked. A 3-tier FTS fallback escalates automatically when results are weak.
   513	
   514	&nbsp;
   515	#### How the Code Graph Works
   516	
   517	The Code Graph is a SQLite-backed structural index that ships as part of the Spec Kit MCP server (`context-server.ts`). It is available to **every supported CLI** - Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and GitHub Copilot - because each runtime connects to the same MCP server via its own config (`.claude/mcp.json`, `.mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.agents/mcp.json`).
   518	
   519	**Startup injection.** When the MCP server starts, it initializes the `code-graph.sqlite` database, runs a non-blocking startup scan, and activates a file watcher. All four supported runtimes (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI) transport the same compact startup shared-payload through their runtime hooks (`session-prime.ts` on Claude/Gemini/Copilot, `session-start.ts` on Codex). The payload includes a one-line health summary, `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment summary), and the `sharedPayloadTransport` envelope so downstream consumers receive identical structural context regardless of runtime. `session_bootstrap()` remains available as a manual recovery surface when native hooks are disabled.
   520	
   521	**Auto-indexing.** The graph stays current through three mechanisms:
   522	1. **Startup scan** - indexes on server boot (async, non-blocking)
   523	2. **File watcher** - Chokidar monitors spec and source folders with a 2-second debounce, reindexing changed files in real time
   524	3. **Lazy refresh** - `code_graph_query` calls `ensureCodeGraphReady()` which detects staleness and triggers a bounded inline refresh before returning results
   525	
   526	The indexer uses tree-sitter to parse source files and extract functions, classes, imports, and call relationships. It tracks per-file content hashes to skip unchanged files, making incremental scans fast.
   527	
   528	&nbsp;
   529	#### Readiness & Response Contract
   530	
   531	`code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context` share a readiness-aware response contract. When the graph is fresh enough, both return `status: "ok"` with resolved results plus a `readiness` / `canonicalReadiness` / `trustState` block. When readiness requires a full scan that cannot run inline, both return an explicit **`status: "blocked"`** payload naming `requiredAction: "code_graph_scan"`, `blockReason: "full_scan_required"`, `degraded`, and `graphAnswersOmitted` instead of silently returning empty results. Callers should run `code_graph_scan` before retrying.
   532	
   533	Success payloads of `code_graph_context` carry structured `data.metadata.partialOutput` (`isPartial`, `reasons`, `omittedSections`, `omittedAnchors`, `truncatedText`) and an explicit `deadlineMs` field so callers can distinguish a complete answer from one trimmed by deadline or budget pressure. `code_graph_status` exposes `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment confidence). CALLS queries on ambiguous subjects (e.g. `handle*`) prefer callable implementation nodes over wrapper-shadow candidates, and return ambiguity / selected-candidate metadata so callers can audit the choice.
   534	
   535	&nbsp;
   536	#### Edge Explanations and Better Blast Radius
   537	
   538	Relationship answers from `code_graph_query` include short `reason` and `step` fields alongside confidence and provenance, so you can see *why* an edge is there instead of just *that* it exists. `code_graph_context` carries those same fields through to structured edges and text briefs.
   539	
   540	`blast_radius` keeps the prior payload (affected files, source files, hot files, multi-file union, depth) and adds:
   541	
   542	- **`depthGroups`** — affected nodes bucketed by how far they sit from the change
   543	- **`riskLevel`** — `high` when the subject is ambiguous or fans out to more than 10 things at depth one, `medium` for 4–10, `low` otherwise
   544	- **`minConfidence`** filter — drop traversals below a confidence floor
   545	- **`ambiguityCandidates`** — list of plausible matches when the subject can't be resolved
   546	- **`failureFallback`** — structured info instead of a bare error string when resolution can't continue
   547	
   548	All of this rides inside the existing `code_edges.metadata` JSON blob — no SQLite schema changes.
   549	
   550	&nbsp;
   551	#### `detect_changes` — Preflight Impact Check
   552	
   553	`detect_changes` is a read-only Code Graph tool that takes a diff and tells you which symbols and files it touches. It runs alongside `code_graph_scan`, `code_graph_query`, `code_graph_status`, and `code_graph_context`.
   554	
   555	You hand it `{ diff: string, rootDir?: string }`. It walks each diff hunk, overlaps the line ranges with stored symbols, and returns `{ status, affectedSymbols[], affectedFiles[], blockedReason?, timestamp, readiness }`.
   556	
   557	Safety is non-negotiable: the tool checks the graph is fresh before parsing the diff. If the graph is stale or unavailable, it returns `status: 'blocked'` immediately — you never get a false "nothing impacted" answer from an out-of-date index. Inline indexing is explicitly disabled here, so the read-only contract is enforced.
   558	
   559	Under the hood the scan runner is split into four declared phases (`find-candidates` → `parse-candidates` → `finalize` → `emit-metrics`) for clearer instrumentation, with no SQLite schema changes.
   560	
   561	&nbsp;
   562	#### What Each System Does
   563	
   564	| System | Best for | Primary surface |
   565	|-------|----------|-----------------|
   566	| **CocoIndex** | Concept search, similar implementations, unfamiliar modules | `mcp__cocoindex_code__search` |
   567	| **Code Graph** | Callers, imports, symbol outlines, impact analysis, neighborhood expansion | `code_graph_scan`, `code_graph_query`, `code_graph_status`, `code_graph_context` |
   568	| **Session bridge tools** | Session bootstrap, resume, and health checks around graph availability | `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `session_health` |
   569	| **CCC utilities** | CocoIndex availability, reindexing, result feedback | `ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, `ccc_feedback` |
   570	
   571	&nbsp;
   572	#### How Query Routing Works (Graph-First)
   573	
   574	The default routing order is: **Code Graph** (structural) -> **CocoIndex** (semantic code) -> **Memory** (session/decision context). This graph-first approach tries structural resolution before semantic similarity, with a 3-tier FTS fallback when earlier stages miss.
   575	
   576	- Use the **Code Graph** first for structural questions: callers, callees, imports, hierarchy, file outlines, and reverse impact.
   577	- Use **CocoIndex** for semantic and intent-based questions: "find code that validates memory quality", "show similar routing patterns", "where is the logic for X?"
   578	- Use **session tools** when recovering or checking environment readiness, but treat `/spec_kit:resume` as the canonical operator-facing recovery surface.
   579	- Rebuild task continuity in this order: `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> canonical spec docs.
   580	- Use **Memory** after those packet-local sources when the question is about prior decisions, spec history, handovers, or task continuity that still needs deeper retrieval.
   581	
   582	&nbsp;
   583	#### Why It Matters
   584	
   585	This split avoids forcing one search system to do everything poorly. Semantic search is good at resemblance. Structural search is good at relationships. Keeping both lets the framework move from "this code looks relevant" to "this is how it connects" without collapsing those concerns into a single noisy result set.
   586

 succeeded in 0ms:
   566	Context preservation across sessions via 5-channel hybrid retrieval (vector, FTS5, BM25, graph, and degree) with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, intent-aware routing, and post-fusion reranking/filtering.
   567	
   568	**Server:** `@spec-kit/mcp-server` v1.7.2 - `context-server.ts` with 51 MCP tools across 7 layers. The tool surface is defined in `mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts`.
   569	
   570	**Memory Commands:** 4 memory slash commands (`/memory:save`, `/memory:manage`, `/memory:learn`, `/memory:search`) cover the spec-doc record command surface, while `/spec_kit:resume` owns session recovery through the broader memory/session recovery stack. The `spec_kit` surface now uses `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only` as the standalone intake workflow; `/spec_kit:plan` and `/spec_kit:complete` execute the shared intake contract (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/intake-contract.md`) inline when the Step 0 local `folder_state` shows repair or creation is needed, and downstream callers should consume the contract's canonical `start_state` rather than reusing the local classifier name. `/spec_kit:deep-research` follows `../sk-deep-research/references/spec_check_protocol.md` for bounded `spec.md` anchoring. The `/memory:search` command covers all analysis and retrieval workflows. See `.opencode/command/memory/`, `.opencode/command/spec_kit/plan.md`, `.opencode/command/spec_kit/complete.md`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/intake-contract.md`, and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md` for command documentation.
   571	
   572	**MCP Tools (18 most-used of 47 total - see [memory_system.md](./references/memory/memory_system.md) for full reference):**
   573	
   574	| Tool                            | Layer | Purpose                                           |
   575	| ------------------------------- | ----- | ------------------------------------------------- |
   576	| `memory_context()`              | L1    | Unified entry point - modes: auto, quick, deep, focused, resume |
   577	| `memory_search()`               | L2    | 5-channel hybrid retrieval with intent-aware routing, channel normalization, graph/degree signals, reranking, and filtered output |
   578	| `memory_quick_search()`         | L2    | Simplified search (query + optional spec folder)  |
   579	| `memory_match_triggers()`       | L2    | Trigger matching + cognitive (decay, tiers, co-activation) |
   580	| `memory_save()`                 | L2    | Index a saved continuity artifact or spec doc with pre-flight validation |
   581	| `memory_list()`                 | L3    | Browse stored spec-doc records with pagination (parent rows by default) |
   582	| `memory_delete()`               | L4    | Delete memories by ID or spec folder              |
   583	| `checkpoint_create()`           | L5    | Create gzip-compressed checkpoint snapshot        |
   584	| `checkpoint_restore()`          | L5    | Transaction-wrapped restore with rollback         |
   585	| `memory_stats()`                | L3    | System statistics and memory counts                |
   586	| `memory_health()`              | L3    | Diagnostics: orphan detection, index consistency   |
   587	| `memory_index_scan()`          | L7    | Workspace scanning and re-indexing                 |
   588	| `checkpoint_list()`            | L5    | List available checkpoint snapshots                |
   589	| `checkpoint_delete()`          | L5    | Delete checkpoint by name (with confirmName safety)|
   590	
   591	> **Search architecture:** The search pipeline uses a 4-stage architecture (candidate generation → fusion → reranking → filtering). Current retrieval uses five channels, normalizes fallback thresholds correctly, keeps disabled channels disabled through fallback, defers irreversible confidence truncation until after reranking, and enforces token budgets using actual post-truncation counts. Adaptive fusion includes an internal continuity profile (`semantic 0.52`, `keyword 0.18`, `recency 0.07`, `graph 0.23`), Stage 3 uses a minimum rerank gate of 4 candidates, the retained `applyLengthPenalty` input is a compatibility no-op, and `getRerankerStatus()` exposes reranker cache hits, misses, stale hits, and evictions. See [search/README.md](./mcp_server/lib/search/README.md) for pipeline details, scoring algorithms, and graph signal features.
   592	
   593	**memory_context() - Mode Routing:**
   594	
   595	| Mode | Token Budget | When `mode=auto`: Intent Routing |
   596	| --- | --- | --- |
   597	| `quick` | 800 | - |
   598	| `deep` | 3500 | `add_feature`, `refactor`, `security_audit` |
   599	| `focused` | 3000 | `fix_bug`, `understand` |
   600	| `resume` | 1200 | - |
   601	
   602	**memory_search() - Key Rules:**
   603	- **REQUIRED:** `query` (string) OR `concepts` (2-5 strings). `specFolder` alone causes E040 error.
   604	- Use `anchors` with `includeContent: true` for token-efficient section retrieval (~90% savings).
   605	- Intent weights auto-adjust scoring: `fix_bug` boosts recency, `security_audit` boosts importance, `refactor`/`understand` boost similarity.
   606	- **Full parameter reference:** See [memory_system.md](./references/memory/memory_system.md)
   607	
   775	**Architecture:** CocoIndex (semantic, external MCP) finds code by concept. Code Graph (structural, this system) maps imports, calls, hierarchy. Memory (session, existing MCP) preserves decisions. The compact-merger combines all three under a 4000-token budget for compaction injection.
   776	
   777	**Budget allocator floors:** constitutional 700, codeGraph 1200, cocoIndex 900, triggered 400, overflow pool 800.
   778	
   779	**Source:** `mcp_server/code_graph/lib/` | `mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/`
   780	
   781	**Parser:** Tree-sitter WASM is the default structural parser for JS/TS/Python/Shell. Set `SPECKIT_PARSER=regex` for regex fallback. Parser adapter pattern allows future language additions.
   782	
   783	**Edge types:** CONTAINS, CALLS, IMPORTS, EXPORTS, EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, DECORATES, OVERRIDES, TYPE_OF.
   784	
   785	**Read-path freshness:** Startup and bootstrap surfaces report graph freshness without mutating the index. Bounded inline refresh happens on structural read paths when stale sets are small; otherwise callers receive `readiness` guidance to run `code_graph_scan`.
   786	
   787	**Blocked/degraded contract:** when readiness requires a suppressed `full_scan`, both `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context` return an explicit `status: "blocked"` payload with `requiredAction: "code_graph_scan"`, `blockReason: "full_scan_required"`, `degraded`, and `graphAnswersOmitted` instead of silently returning empty results. This is uniform across query and context reads.
   788	
   789	**Context metadata:** `code_graph_context` success payloads carry structured `data.metadata.partialOutput` (`isPartial`, `reasons`, `omittedSections`, `omittedAnchors`, `truncatedText`) plus an explicit `deadlineMs` field so callers can distinguish a complete answer from one trimmed by deadline or budget pressure. `code_graph_status` exposes `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment confidence). CocoIndex seed fidelity preserves `score`, `snippet`, and range metadata through context resolution.
   790	
   791	**CALLS disambiguation:** `code_graph_query` CALLS mode on ambiguous subjects (e.g. `handle*`) prefers callable implementation nodes over wrapper-shadow candidates, and records ambiguity / selected-candidate metadata so callers can audit the choice.
   792	
   793	**Startup payload parity:** all four runtimes (Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Codex) transport the same compact startup shared-payload through their runtime-specific startup hooks, including the `graphQualitySummary` summary. `session_bootstrap()` remains available as a manual recovery surface when native hooks are disabled.
   794	
   795	`lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts` is now the shared readiness source for query, scan, status, context, and CCC handlers. Its readiness blocks project onto the canonical `SharedPayloadTrustState` type instead of introducing a new local trust-state enum.
   796	
   797	**Query routing:** Structural queries (callers, imports, deps) -> `code_graph_query`. Semantic/concept queries -> CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`). Session/memory queries -> `memory_context`.
   798	
   799	**CocoIndex seed bridge:** CocoIndex search results (file + line range) are accepted as seeds by `code_graph_context`, which resolves them to graph nodes and expands structurally. This combines "what resembles what" (CocoIndex) with "what connects to what" (Code Graph).
   800	
   801	**CCC tools** (CocoIndex lifecycle):

codex
Search system has three layers, not one:

1. **Spec Kit Memory search**
   `memory_context()` is the preferred entry point, with `memory_search()` as the lower-level hybrid retrieval API. It uses vector, FTS5, BM25, causal graph, and degree channels, then runs 4 stages: candidate generation, fusion, rerank, filtering.
   See [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/README.md:363), [SKILL.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:566), and [search README](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:36).

2. **Code Graph**
   Structural search: callers, imports, outlines, blast radius, graph neighborhoods. Default routing says structural queries go here before semantic search.
   See [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/README.md:508) and [SKILL.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md:775).

3. **CocoIndex**
   Semantic code search: “find code that does X”, similar implementations, unfamiliar modules. It bridges into Code Graph via seed results, but current session startup reported CocoIndex as missing.
   See [mcp-coco-index/SKILL.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/SKILL.md:10) and [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/README.md:562).

Core code entry points:

- [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts:13): unified retrieval routing, intent detection, Code Graph context hooks.
- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:11): main `memory_search` handler, pipeline execution, telemetry, cache, feedback.
- [orchestrator.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/orchestrator.ts:46): 4-stage pipeline controller.
- [stage1-candidate-gen.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage1-candidate-gen.ts:6): raw retrieval from vector, hybrid, multi-concept, expansion paths.
- [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts:6): single scoring point for session boost, recency, causal boost, graph signals, intent weights, feedback.

Relevant spec packets:

- [010-search-retrieval-quality-fixes](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/010-search-retrieval-quality-fixes/implementation-summary.md:40): fixed retrieval quality around context routing, folder recovery, token budgets, and intent confidence.
- [013-fts5-fix-and-search-dashboard](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/013-fts5-fix-and-search-dashboard/implementation-summary.md:45): fixed FTS5 multi-word quoting, DB path drift, search dashboard grouping, and silent failure logging.
- [026 graph/context optimization](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-code-graph/implementation-summary.md): broader Code Graph and routing work.

My read: the live architecture is “graph-first for structure, CocoIndex for semantic code, Memory for session/spec history,” with Memory search internally using a 5-channel hybrid pipeline.
